direction.description: The QoS 2 handshake relies on Packet Identifier state; duplicate or incomplete handshakes must not break state lifecycle.

spec_ref.entity: Packet Identifier and QoS 2 transaction state
spec_ref.quote_or_summary: QoS 2 uses a multi-step flow to provide exactly-once delivery.

statement: Duplicate QoS 2 Packet Identifiers and incomplete QoS 2 handshakes must not create unbounded duplicate state or leak transaction resources.
condition: A client sends repeated QoS 2 messages with duplicate Packet Identifiers and does not complete the expected handshake.
expected_behavior: The broker deduplicates, rejects, replaces, or cleans up state according to the protocol state machine.
violation_pattern: The broker accumulates unreleased state for duplicate/incomplete QoS 2 transactions.
bound_goal: Exactly-once delivery and resource lifecycle consistency

review.review_notes: May be downgraded if root cause is purely EAGAIN/memory management.
